name: Declaration of War — War of 1812
notes: First declared war. Closest war vote in US history. Federalists opposed.

name: Declaration of War — Mexican-American War
notes: Abraham Lincoln (then a Congressman) challenged Polk's justification with his "Spot Resolutions."

name: Declaration of War — Spanish-American War
notes: Launched American empire. US gained Philippines, Puerto Rico, Guam, Cuba.

name: Declaration of War — World War I
notes: Jeannette Rankin (first woman in Congress) voted no. She also voted against WWII — the only member to vote against both.

name: Declaration of War — World War II (Japan)
notes: Only dissenting vote: Jeannette Rankin (R-MT). "As a woman I can't go to war, and I refuse to send anyone else."

name: Declaration of War — World War II (Germany)
notes: Unanimous after Pearl Harbor. Last formal declaration of war by the US.

name: Gulf of Tonkin Resolution
notes: Based on a fabricated attack. Only Wayne Morse (D-OR) and Ernest Gruening (D-AK) voted no. Led to 58,220 American deaths. Repealed in 1971.

name: War Powers Resolution
notes: Requires president to notify Congress within 48 hours of committing forces. Widely ignored by every president since.

name: Authorization — Gulf War (1991)
notes: Close Senate vote. Biden voted NO (then reversed his position for the 2003 Iraq War).

name: Authorization for Use of Military Force (2001 AUMF)
notes: Only Barbara Lee (D-CA) voted no. "Let us not become the evil we deplore." This single vote authorized wars in 22 countries over 20+ years. Still in effect.

name: Authorization — Iraq War (2002 AUMF)
notes: Based on false WMD claims. Biden, Clinton, Kerry all voted yes. 23 senators voted no including Byrd, Kennedy, Leahy, Durbin. Repealed in 2024.

name: Libya — War Powers Challenge
notes: House rebuked Obama for not getting authorization for Libya bombing. Obama argued it wasn't "hostilities." No consequences.

name: Syria Strike Authorization (failed)
notes: Obama sought authorization for Syria strikes after chemical weapons use, then withdrew the request when Congress clearly opposed it.

name: Yemen War Powers Resolution
notes: First use of War Powers Resolution to end US involvement in a conflict. Bipartisan. Trump vetoed it.

name: 2002 AUMF Repeal
notes: Repealed the Iraq War authorization 21 years after passage. Bipartisan. 2001 AUMF still remains in effect.
